cases.current_user_request: Open Hacker News, but keep it compact like last time.
Open the Reddit thread from earlier this week and scroll slower this time.
Do the same cleanup for the second Stripe CSV.

snapshots.current_user_request: Open Hacker News, but keep it compact like last time.

task_choice.reason: The user requests opening Hacker News with a compact layout, which is a direct request for a visual panel. The conversation and session memories already capture the user's preference for dense story rows, so no further information is required.

messages.content: Hacker News worked best as tight rows of titles, scores, and comment counts. The preview cards made it feel too airy.
The user prefers a compact Hacker News layout with title-first rows and visible metadata.
Got it. Hacker News should stay compact, with no expanded preview cards.

session_memories.title: Hacker News row density
session_memories.description: The user likes Hacker News as dense story rows: title first, score and comment count visible, and preview cards avoided.

selected_task.name: Prepare visual panel
selected_task.description: Prepare a visual panel for the user's browsing task.
selected_task.input: The user's request and conversation context.
selected_task.output: A visual panel plan and context needed to render it.
selected_task.prompt: Prepare the visual panel requested by the user.

task_choice.reason: The user wants to reopen a Reddit thread and scroll slower, which is a request to adjust a website view. The available task for preparing a visual panel is appropriate.
task_choice.agent_thought: User is reiterating the desire for slower scrolling, consistent with earlier memory.

candidate_memory.title: Reddit thread earlier this week
candidate_memory.description: The user referred to a Reddit thread read earlier this week. The specific thread identity and URL are not confirmed in memory, only the scrolling pace preference associated with Reddit browsing.

messages.content: The Reddit thread kept moving while I was still reading nested replies.
The user is reacting to browsing motion, not the Reddit content itself.
I will give Reddit threads more time between scrolls.

session_memories.title: Reddit scrolling pace
session_memories.description: The user found Reddit discussion threads hard to read when the panel moved again before they finished nested replies.

snapshots.current_user_request: Do the same cleanup for the second Stripe CSV.

task_choice.reason: The user wants to perform the same duplicate-row cleanup on the second CSV (February), but the agent still lacks the concrete local file path necessary to proceed. The conversation history shows the user has not provided any file path yet, so more information is required.
task_choice.agent_thought: The user is asking to clean the February CSV, but no file path has been provided for either CSV. We need to ask for the path to the February CSV file.

candidate_memory.title: February CSV path still missing
candidate_memory.description: User referred to February as 'the second Stripe CSV' and asked for the same duplicate-row cleanup applied to January. The February file path has not yet been provided.

messages.content: I downloaded January and February CSV exports from Stripe. January has duplicate rows after yesterday's export hiccup.
The user named two files conceptually but has not provided local paths.
I can clean January first once you give me the file path, then apply the same duplicate-row cleanup to February.

session_memories.title: Stripe export pair
session_memories.description: The active cleanup conversation involves January and February Stripe CSV exports. January was described as having duplicate rows after an export hiccup.

candidate_memory.title: Compact Hacker News is a settled visual-panel preference
candidate_memory.description: The user consistently asks for Hacker News in a compact, title-first row layout with visible score and comment counts and no preview cards. Since the preference has been captured in a session memory, follow-up requests referencing "last time" or "like before" can lean on recent context without deep historical searches.

candidate_memory.title: Reddit scrolling preference established from earlier this week
candidate_memory.description: The user references a Reddit thread from earlier this week and asks to 'scroll slower this time.' This connects to the Reddit reading motion conversation (2026-06-30) where the user complained that nested replies kept moving before they finished reading, prompting the agent to commit to slower scroll speeds for Reddit threads.

candidate_memory.title: Followup requests with 'the second' need wider conversation window
candidate_memory.description: When a user says 'do the same' or 'the second [item]', they are referencing prior session context that may be older than the current session. Using a one-week window for past_conversation_context helps locate the earlier discussion and confirm what 'the second' refers to, even when the current session already contains relevant context.

candidate_memory.title: Compact-panel references benefit from deeper conversation search
candidate_memory.description: Requests that include 'like last time' or reference prior layout preferences should search past conversations broadly (roughly two weeks back) to catch earlier session contexts beyond the current session.

candidate_memory.title: Reddit thread earlier this week with scrolling complaint
candidate_memory.description: User references a Reddit thread read earlier this week. A prior session on 2026-06-30 noted Reddit nested replies were hard to follow because the visual panel scrolled too quickly. The specific thread URL is not yet confirmed in memory; searching past conversations from late June may reveal it.

candidate_memory.title: February Stripe CSV cleanup pending file path
candidate_memory.description: User asked to apply the same duplicate-row cleanup to the February Stripe CSV ('the second one'). The February file path has not yet been provided, just as January needed a path earlier. Past sessions may contain the January cleanup steps that should be replicated.

candidate_memory.title: Search depth for 'last time' follow-up requests with established preferences
candidate_memory.description: When a user references 'like last time' and the current session already contains the relevant preference, use moderately old cutoffs for past conversation context (about 2-3 weeks back) to catch any earlier sessions where the preference was first formed, while the current session itself already provides the most relevant context. Task catalog cutoffs can be older since those records change slowly.

candidate_memory.title: Reddit scrolling pace preference from earlier this week
candidate_memory.description: On 2026-06-30 the user reported that Reddit thread panels kept moving before they finished reading nested replies. The agent committed to slowing scroll pace. The current request to 'scroll slower this time' references that same session, so earlier-this-week conversation context around June 28–July 1 should be searched to locate the thread and support the visual panel preparation.

candidate_memory.title: February CSV cleanup is a followup to January
candidate_memory.description: The 'second Stripe CSV' refers to the February export. The user expects the same duplicate-row cleanup applied to January to be repeated for February. Both file paths were requested but only the path for January may have been provided in an earlier session; the February path is still needed to proceed.

candidate_memory.title: Compact Hacker News preference persists across sessions
candidate_memory.description: The user consistently asks for Hacker News in a compact, title-first row layout with visible score and comment counts, avoiding preview cards. Requests referencing 'last time' for visual panel preferences benefit from searching conversation context at least two weeks back to catch prior session discussions.

candidate_memory.title: Reddit thread earlier this week and slow-scroll preference
candidate_memory.description: User requested opening a Reddit thread from earlier this week with slower scrolling, referencing a past session on 2026-06-30 where they reported that nested replies were hard to read because the panel scrolled too quickly. The specific thread URL was not confirmed in memory.

candidate_memory.title: February CSV path still missing
candidate_memory.description: User referred to February as 'the second Stripe CSV' and asked for the same duplicate-row cleanup applied to January. The February file path has not yet been provided.

candidate_memory.description: The user referred to a Reddit thread from earlier this week (around June 30 - July 2). The specific thread identity and URL are not confirmed in memory. The user prefers slower scrolling when reading nested Reddit replies. Search past session conversations from the start of the week to identify the thread.
